summary: Full E2E testing of ChatMaroc completed. All 9 backend pytest tests passed (root, languages, SSE chat, message persistence/order, clear, sign-language upload + empty validation, transcribe reachability). All targeted frontend flows verified via Playwright on the public URL: empty-state + 3 suggestions, language selector exposes darija/tamazight-rif/tamazight-atlas, text send produces streaming non-empty assistant reply with typing indicator/SSE deltas, suggestion click triggers a chat round-trip, clear-chat returns to empty state, sign-language modal opens and shows the camera-denied error gracefully (no webcam in test env), and the modal closes via the close button. No bugs found.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: logger is defined AFTER it is referenced (line 147 uses logger inside event_generator; logger configured at line 240). Works at runtime because the function is called after module init, but reordering logging.basicConfig + getLogger above the routes is cleaner.

Minor: /api/transcribe writes uploaded bytes to disk then re-opens for STT but never deletes the temp audio file (same for /api/process-sign-language webm uploads). Consider periodic cleanup or storing in a temp dir if persistence isn't required.
Minor: SSE writes assistant content only after stream completes; if client disconnects mid-stream partial reply is lost (acceptable for MVP).
Minor: get_messages uses .to_list(1000) hard cap — consider pagination for long sessions.
seed_data_creation: Tests use TEST_<uuid> session_id and clean up via DELETE /api/messages/{session_id} in module teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_chatmaroc_api.py cover all 6 endpoints. SSE stream test consumes data: chunks and waits for done:true. Sign-language endpoint accepts video/webm mime; empty body returns 400. Whisper transcribe test only checks reachability + JSON shape because dummy bytes legitimately return 500. Frontend webcam is unavailable in Playwright headless — sign-language modal correctly surfaces sign-language-error block.
